THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO ALLOW THE LATE FILING OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. THERE HAS BEEN NO SIGNIFICANT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANTS AND THE LAW FAVORS DECISIONS ON THE MERITS. LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT IRENE TANG'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST, FIFTH, SIXTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION ONE AND ELEVEN TO EITHER STATE A CLAIM NOT BASED ON A RIGHT TO REMAIN IN THE HOUSE OR TO PLEAD AROUND THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 5 AND 6 TO PLEAD THAT ELDER ABUSE HAS OCCURRED AND FACTS THAT MEET THE ELEMENTS OF INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

ATTY. ROGER L. MEREDITH, TEL. NO. 616-5959 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT IRENE TANG'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST, FIFTH, SIXTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION ONE AND ELEVEN TO EITHER STATE A CLAIM NOT BASED ON A RIGHT TO REMAIN IN THE HOUSE OR TO PLEAD AROUND THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS.
